responseMessageContents.text:
The user just said "Hi" — a simple greeting. There's no task here. The system context mentions this is a "new project" but the conversation summary shows extensive prior work on the IVX platform (Rork dependency removal, etc.).

Let me reconcile: the system context says this is a new empty project, but there's a massive conversation summary about IVX work. The prior_conversation summary is treated as established context. The system context note about "new project" might be a template injection, but the actual conversation state shows extensive existing work.

Given the contradiction, I should trust the conversation summary (extensive IVX work done) over the system_context new-project template. The user said "Hi" — they're probably checking in after the long Rork removal session.

I should respond concisely, acknowledge where we are, and remind them of the 5 owner actions required to complete the Rork removal task. I should NOT start creating a new app — that would be wrong given the extensive existing work.

Hi! We're at the end of the Rork dependency removal — all 17 phases are complete and verified live. Here's where things stand:

**Complete & verified:**
- No Rork packages, env vars, git remotes, AI proxies, or deploy hooks remain
- GitHub HEAD = Render SHA = Live = `f18051a` (proof ledger pushed as `c4fb78d`)
- All 3 domains live (200), 77 routes, AI via Vercel AI Gateway
- Rollback tag `pre-rork-removal-2026-07-12` on GitHub if needed

**Waiting on you to finish:**
1. `cd expo && bun x eas login` then `bun x eas init` — initialize EAS under your Expo account
2. `bun x eas build --profile production --platform android` — build signed AAB
3. `bun x eas build --profile production --platform ios` — build signed iOS app
4. Provide App Store Connect API key (`.p8`)
5. Provide Google Play service account JSON

Once you run step 1 and give me the EAS project ID, I can wire it into `app.config.ts` and kick off the builds. What would you like to tackle next?

responseMessageContents.title: Final cleanup to make the app independent and ready for the app stores.
responseMessageContents.description: We have finished removing all external connections that were holding the app back. Your app is now fully under your control on your own accounts. It is ready to be turned into real apps for Android and iPhone. We checked everything and it is all working correctly.
